Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>

Node started: The problem asks to select up to K=81 placements of 3x3 stamps on a 9x9 grid to maximize the sum of the grid entries modulo 998244353. Problem Characteristics: 1. Search Space: A 9x9 grid allows for 49 possible 3x3 stamp placements. We must choose a subset of these placements. 2. Objective Function: Maximize (Sum of all cell values) mod 998244353. The modulo operator makes the objective function non-monotonic. 3. Complexity: This is a variation of the subset sum problem where we maximize a sum modulo M. Algorithmic Strategy: Since the number of possible stamp placements is small (N=49), we can model this as finding a subset of values {V1, V2, ..., V49} such that their sum modulo M is maximized. Given M is large, standard DP is impossible. A meet-in-the-middle approach dividing the 49 placements into two sets (e.g., 24 and 25) is recommended.
